context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the convention for infinite scaling limits
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The rule defining how to handle infinity as a threshold value in average calculations, requiring a codebase search for 'Infinity handling' or 'unbounded thresholds'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: reference the specific neutral error protocol
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The logic for returning a 'not_found' error when instant precedes all parameters, located in the error handling section for Parameter Lookup.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_period_parse.json.description: Parse a compact period expression into its unit, first instant and length, reporting a neutral error category and echoing the rejected selector for malformed expressions.

feature1_2_period_format.json.description: Render a period given by its unit, first instant and length back to its canonical short string form.

feature1_3_period_subdivision.json.description: Expand a period into the ordered, inclusive list of smaller periods of a requested unit.

feature2_1_amount_scale.json.description: Apply a fixed-amount bracket scale: each base value accumulates the amounts of every bracket whose threshold it reaches; values below the first threshold yield zero.

feature2_2_marginal_charge.json.description: Apply a marginal-rate bracket scale to a base vector, with optional rounding of the base to a given number of decimals before the brackets are applied.

feature2_3_average_view.json.description: Convert a marginal-rate scale into its equivalent average-rate view, exposing the average thresholds, average rates, and the average-rate liability for a base vector.

feature2_4_inverse_recovery.json.description: Given a marginal-rate scale, derive net values from gross values and verify the inverse scale recovers the original gross from the net.

feature2_5_combine_scales.json.description: Stack several marginal-rate scales (each given as dated brackets) evaluated at an instant into a single merged scale, reporting the union of thresholds and the summed rates.

feature3_average_rate.json.description: Compute the element-wise average rate of a target vector against a varying denominator, emitting a neutral infinity marker when the denominator is zero.

feature4_threshold_choices.json.description: Select, for each input value, the choice matching the first threshold band it falls into; thresholds may be scalar or per-position vectors, and an invalid threshold/choice cardinality is a neutral error.

feature5_parameter_lookup.json.description: Resolve a dated scalar parameter from a parameter tree at a requested instant, returning the value in force; a request before a parameter exists or after it is stopped is a neutral not-found error.

feature6_1_household_membership.json.description: From a situation of persons and households (each grouping members as parents and children), derive the ordered person and household identifiers and, per person, the owning household index, role key, legacy role number and position within the household.

feature6_2_situation_validation.json.description: Validate and normalise a list-form situation: auto-assign missing identifiers and required group entities, coerce a single member id into a list, and report a neutral error for malformed situations.

feature6_3_entity_values.json.description: Read a stored group-entity variable from a structured situation at a requested period, returning the values in household order.

feature7_1_variable_calculation.json.description: Calculate a dated variable for a single household from declared monthly inputs: a direct read, a year-total aggregation that sums months, a month-share aggregation that prorates a yearly value, a progressive-scale derived variable, and a neutral not-found error for an unknown variable.

feature7_2_axis_expansion.json.description: Expand a base household along one or more axes that interpolate a member input between a minimum and maximum over a fixed count of steps, then evaluate a variable over the generated grid.

feature7_3_enum_input.json.description: Accept an enumerated household status supplied either as a label or as a numeric code through the input mechanism, and read it back as a stable numeric code.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a string
_constraints.output_rule: Parse unit/style; if year only, output length=1; if month only, output length=1; if year:YYYY-MM output length=1; if year:YYYY-MM:N or month:YYYY-MM:N output length=N

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a year like '2014' without unit prefix
_constraints.output_rule: Treat as year starting Jan 1, length 1

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a month like '2014-01' without unit prefix
_constraints.output_rule: Treat as month starting 1st, length 1

_constraints.input_condition: Unit is 'year', start is Jan, size is 1
_constraints.output_rule: Collapse to year only

_constraints.input_condition: Unit is 'month', start is Jan, size is 12
_constraints.output_rule: Collapse to year only

_constraints.input_condition: Unit is 'month', start is not Jan, size is 12
_constraints.output_rule: Output as year:start-month (explicit unit required)

_constraints.input_condition: Input period is 'YYYY' and requested unit is 'month'
_constraints.output_rule: Subdivide into 12 months, list starts 01 and ends 12

_constraints.input_condition: Input period is 'year:YYYY:N' and requested unit is 'month'
_constraints.output_rule: Subdivide into N*12 months covering the specified range, list starts Jan and ends Dec of claim year+N

_constraints.input_condition: Input uses fixed-amount brackets (threshold + amount)
_constraints.output_rule: Liability is sum of amounts for all brackets where base >= threshold

_constraints.input_condition: Input uses marginal-rate brackets (threshold + rate)
_constraints.output_rule: Liability is sum of (portion_in_bracket * rate) for all brackets where base >= threshold

_constraints.input_condition: Base contains floating point numbers
_constraints.output_rule: Apply brackets element-wise using standard floating point arithmetic as per test cases

_constraints.input_condition: Input uses marginal-rate brackets
_constraints.output_rule: Convert to average thresholds/rates: avg_thresholds includes "Infinity" at end; avg_rates computed accordingly; avg_liability applies new scale

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides marginal-scale and a 'gross' vector
_constraints.output_rule: Compute 'net' = gross - charge(gross); Create inverse scale; Recover 'gross' from 'net' = inverse_constant(net) + charge_constant(net)

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides multiple scales with 'instant' key '2015'
_constraints.output_rule: Union thresholds; sum rates at common thresholds; exclude thresholds only present in one scale

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides 'target' and scalar 'varying'
_constraints.output_rule: Element-wise target/varying; if varying is 0, output "-Infinity"

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides scalar thresholds and vector choices
_constraints.output_rule: Assign each value to first threshold where value >= threshold; output corresponding choice

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides scalar thresholds and fewer choices than values + valid thresholds
_constraints.output_rule: Assign remaining unmatched values to the last known choice (0 if first unmatched value >= last threshold)

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides vector thresholds (list-of-lists) - deprecated?
_constraints.output_rule: Test case shows 2D thresholds. Assign based on first matching threshold in list? Output: [0,1,1]

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides parameter tree with 'values' keyed by date ISO string
_constraints.output_rule: Find key <= instant (exclusive? Tests show 2014-06-01 returns 2014-01-01 value). Return value.

_constraints.input_condition: Input instant is before all provided values
_constraints.output_rule: Neutral error

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides named persons and neighborhoods with parent/child lists
_constraints.output_rule: Output ordered arrays of persons, household IDs, and role strings; legacy_role_by_person is 0-indexed within all households combined

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides elderlyHousehold (ages) list
_constraints.output_rule: Append ages to output arrays

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides list-form households with scalar parent reference
_constraints.output_rule: Coerce scalar parent ID to array of length 1

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides situation with nested period data
_constraints.output_rule: Return values of variable for specific period as ordered list

_constraints.input_condition: Input specifies 'aggregation: year_total' with year period
_constraints.output_rule: Sum all months of year specified in 'salary' input

_constraints.input_condition: Input specifies 'aggregation: month_share' with year period
_constraints.output_rule: Divide year total by 12

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides axes with 'count', 'min', 'max'
_constraints.output_rule: Generate N equidistant steps between min and max

_constraints.input_condition: Input provides label string for variable
_constraints.output_rule: Convert label to stable numeric code

_constraints.input_condition: All outputs
_constraints.output_rule: Line breaks are literal `\n`; numbers are rendered compactly (integers as ints, floats rounded to 10 decimals); errors are neutral strings
